# Feed-relative paths as query sources: path suggestions + Copy path

## Why
When picking the CSV that feeds a `csvsql` `<input>` (or the `.xlsx` source of `xlsx2csv`), the
designer's path autocomplete only offered the `${var}` builtins and the feed's **root** uploads —
not the files **generated by the workflow** (the run outputs such as
`10_SQL_EXTRACTION/tf0003819_SQL_EXTRACTION.csv`, `20_ANONYMIZATION/...csv`, etc.). Those are exactly
what a downstream `csvsql`/`xlsx2csv` wants to read. Two changes make them first-class sources.

## 1. Path suggestions include generated files
`templates/designer.html`:
- `loadFeedDocs()` now fetches the **full** feed file list (`/files`, dropping `?root=true`), so the
  run-generated outputs (kind `output`, in step subfolders) are added to the `pathsug` suggestions as
  `${feedDir}/<relative-path>` — searchable in every path field (csvsql input, xlsx2csv source,
  csvFile, validate source, etc.). Scripts stay excluded (referenced via their `${alias}`).
- The embedded WORKFLOW FILES panel still lists **root** uploads only (it is the upload panel); its
  onLoad callback now just calls `loadFeedDocs()` so suggestions refresh from the full list after an
  upload/create. (Single owner of `FEED_DOCS` → no root/full race.)

## 2. "Copy path" button in the Feed Files panel
`static/js/filespanel.js`:
- Each file row gets a **Copy path** button that copies the **feed-relative** path (e.g.
  `10_SQL_EXTRACTION/tf0003819_SQL_EXTRACTION.csv`), not the absolute `G:\...` path. Uses
  `navigator.clipboard` with an `execCommand('copy')` textarea fallback for old Edge/IE; shows a brief
  "Copied!" confirmation. Actions column widened to fit.

## 3. Bare relative paths resolve against ${feedDir}
So a copied feed-relative path works directly as a source (no manual `${feedDir}/` prefixing):
- `engine/InternalSteps.rebaseRel(path, vars)` — if the resolved path is **not absolute**, rebase it
  against `${feedDir}`; absolute (incl. `${var}`-expanded) paths are untouched. Applied to every
  `csvsql` `<input>` path and to the `xlsx2csv` source.
- `web/ApiController.rebaseRel(...)` — same rule, applied in the `csvsql` preview and the `xlsx`
  sheets/preview endpoints so previews match the executor.

## Notes
- `${feedDir}/<path>` (from suggestions) and a bare `<path>` (from Copy path) now both resolve to the
  same file; absolute and `${landingOut}/...` style paths are unchanged.
- `_logs` / `_runs` remain excluded from the listing; `.tmp` and `_assets.json` are skipped.

## Verification (sandbox)
- Full compile: 96 classes (against the Spring/Jackson/jt400/POI stubs).
- `node --check` passes for designer inline JS and `filespanel.js`; no literal `\n`/`\r`; no unsafe
  `[[`/`[(`.
- Live browser behaviour (clipboard, the datalist dropdown) and real path resolution are UBS-side
  (Ctrl+F5).
